what_it_does: It is the generative grammar of the design DNA: a JSON rulebook that defines how every length, size, proportion, alignment, colour, shape, texture, motion, and page-quality constraint is derived from a base unit, a signature ratio, dials, and role/context, rather than from hardcoded pixel values. It is intended to be consumed by a resolver to emit concrete design tokens.
observations: Versioned as grammar-10 with layered harvest notes (grammar-3 through grammar-10) showing iterative expansion from tokens, deck harvests, and rule-core work.
States a core principle that values have no inherent identity and are defined by relations, so nothing is hardcoded.
Base unit is 8; the reference frame 1600x900 is only a measurement context, because proportions are fractions.
Signature ratio 1.5, named the fifth, generates both the radius scale and the display-type scale and is tunable within [1.25, 1.618].
Commensurability chain links type leading (~24) to the 8-grid via division by 3.
Space scale uses unit x step with named steps and an extensible address spine of fractional unit counts.
Bonds ladder maps relationship strength (fused, kin, grouped, sectioned, zoned, framed) to unit counts, making spacing derive from bond strength.
Radius and display type scales are geometric progressions of the signature ratio; text sizes are a separate reading set.
Proportions are fractions that sum to ~1, including title-main-footer, split variants, and plate-internal zoning.
Composition is reduced to exactly three relations: nesting, connection, and override.
Resolution rule is nearest-scope-wins, with no specificity or cascade ordering.
Meta principles include relativistic identity, meaning as difference, identity from boundaries, scale invariance, DNA as root, any part is a whole, and the two halves.
Colour relations include equal-lightness zoning, signal economy, accent ramp, ordinal axis, foreign-hue quarantine, and chrome modes.
Dials are warmth, density, register, modality, and polarity; each declares what it modifies and by what transform.
Scale tiers re-base the unit for poster, slide, page, section, card, and component.
Invariants are listed with checker classifications: predicate, model, or build, plus status notes like corpus-direct or derived-from-principle.
Shape semantics assign entity meaning to geometry and enforce rarity (octagon at most once per page/deck).
Movement verbs (transport, gather, descend) use dashed lines, and only movement-layer elements may cross zone seams.
Provision laws state what a page must provide: zones not plates, diagram atmosphere, evidence density, nesting depth, controlled density, fill, and iconography.
rules_corpus captures harvested corpus-level patterns as prose paragraphs.
standouts: The relativistic-identity / nothing-hardcoded principle and the derivation of all values from base unit + signature ratio + dials + context.
A single signature ratio (1.5) generating both radius and display-type scales.
Invariants classified by checker type (predicate, model, build) with explicit provenance/status.
The provision layer added to prevent 80% outputs that obey restraints but lack substance.
The bonds ladder formalising spacing as a function of relationship strength.
conventions: Uses _rule, _class, _note, _what, and _provenance keys to annotate data with rationale and origin.
Classifies rules as Observed or Inferred.
References external files (tokens.json, sequence.json, resolve.mjs, deck harvests) inside provenance strings.
Mixes structured data with long prose values and meta-commentary.
Maps semantic names to exponents for radius and display-type addresses.
Dials declare targets.modifies and a by transform rather than hardcoding dial names in consumers.
concerns: Inline prose and layered harvest notes make the file serve as both data and document, increasing size and drift risk.
Several entries are marked AWAITING, PROPOSAL, SEEDED, or PREDICTED-FROM-PRINCIPLE rather than canonical.
Harvested off-ramp pixel values for radius and type exist alongside ratio-derived scales, creating a reconciliation gap.
Invariants mix predicate, model, and build checkers but no schema defines how each is evaluated.
Some measured vs emitted values differ slightly (e.g., radius xl emits 27 vs measured 26) and are noted but not resolved.
rules_corpus is unstructured prose rather than machine-readable rules.
notes: A self-annotated generative design-grammar file that encodes ratios, scales, composition rules, invariants, and dials, with extensive provenance notes explaining which rules were measured, inferred, or still awaiting canonical approval.
questions: How does resolve.mjs choose between ratio-derived scales and the harvested pixel-preserving mirrors?
What is the canonical admission process for candidate fine spacing counts and bond-level counts?
How are model invariants that require an AI judge actually evaluated in the build/runtime pipeline?
How does the polarity dial interact with depth/heart inversion beyond luminosity?
Which invariants are currently enforced automatically and which remain aspirational?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it is a structured, versioned generative grammar consumed by a resolver, though it also functions as a living design-theory document with extensive commentary and pending proposals.
apparent_themes: Generative derivation from a minimal parametric root
Signal economy across colour, geometry, and gold usage
intention_signals: To turn a visual brand identity into a parametric, tunable rule system so that any artifact can be re-derived from base unit, signature ratio, dials, and context while remaining recognizable, and to preserve the design rationale and provenance alongside the rules.
novelty: A design grammar encoded as JSON with formal invariants, checker classifications, bond-strength spacing, provision laws, and self-documenting provenance notes — treating visual properties as derivable relations rather than token values.
contribution: It contributes a structured, versioned source of generative design rules and quality invariants that a fused system would keep as the canonical reference for resolving spacing, radius, type, color, composition, shape, texture, motion, and page-level constraints, plus its provenance annotations explaining rule origins.
summary_for_embedding: grammar.json is a versioned generative design-grammar document that defines base units, a signature ratio, spacing/radius/type scales, proportional layout archetypes, composition relations, color relations, dials, invariants, shape semantics, movement verbs, and provision laws, with extensive observed/inferred provenance notes.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to encode the design DNA as derivable, tunable rules and constraints so that concrete visual values can be resolved from a small parametric root instead of being hardcoded.
